title: Cinematic Emotion Annotation Schema
description: Formal specification for composer-annotated ground truth labels in the Cinematic Emotion Lab research corpus. Ground truth is established from compositional intent - what the composer was constructing - not from listener emotional response. This distinction is methodologically deliberate and fully documented in docs/METHODOLOGY.md.

annotation_protocol.annotation_target_description: What emotional state the composer was constructing at time of composition. Not listener emotional response. Not post-hoc interpretation. First-person compositional intent, applied at the moment of annotation with reference to the original compositional record.
annotation_protocol.annotation_authority: The composer is the ground truth. Annotations reflect the technical and expressive decisions made during composition, not any subsequent interpretation.
annotation_protocol.inter_annotator_design: Single-annotator corpus (primary researcher as composer). Inter-annotator consistency methodology documented separately in docs/METHODOLOGY.md Phase 2.
annotation_protocol.corpus_status: Full annotation corpus withheld pending publication. Public subset available at datasets/processed/public_subset_features.csv (acoustic features only, labels withheld).

clip_id.description: Unique identifier for each annotated cinematic audio cue. Format: CEL-[year]-[four-digit sequence]. Anonymized in public corpus subset.

primary_emotion.description: The dominant emotional category the composer was constructing in this cue. Chosen from eight categories tuned to cinematic narrative function and expected acoustic separability. Overlap cases (tension/suspense, grief/longing) are documented as edge cases, not collapsed.
primary_emotion.annotation_guidance: Select the single category that best describes the primary emotional construction. If two categories are equally present and unresolvable, select ambiguity and document in composer_notes.

secondary_emotion.description: A secondary emotional layer present in the cue, subordinate to the primary emotion. Null if no meaningful secondary layer is present. Common secondary pairings (e.g., dread + longing, triumph + tension) are tracked as a corpus-level pattern.

emotional_intensity.description: Ordinal scale capturing the magnitude of emotional construction in the cue. Calibrated to dramatic weight and compositional density, not raw loudness. A pianissimo grief cue can score 5. A fortissimo action cue can score 2 if the emotional construction is thin.

scale_definition.1: Minimal emotional construction - sparse, ambient, emotionally uncommitted
scale_definition.2: Understated - present but restrained, deliberate low-profile construction
scale_definition.3: Moderate - clear emotional direction, standard dramatic weight
scale_definition.4: Elevated - high dramatic commitment, emotionally saturated
scale_definition.5: Maximal - peak dramatic intensity, full compositional resources engaged

narrative_position.description: The position of this cue within the larger dramatic arc of the scene or sequence it was composed for. Narrative position is a first-class annotation dimension because the same acoustic features carry different emotional meaning at different points in a dramatic arc.
narrative_position.annotation_guidance: Annotate relative to the immediate scene or sequence context, not the full film. A cue that opens a new scene is 'opening' even if it follows a climax in the prior scene.
narrative_position.research_relevance: RQ4: Does narrative position leave measurable and predictive traces in the acoustic signal? Expected: yes. Controlling for narrative position is hypothesized to improve classification accuracy in EXP-002.
narrative_position.status: field structure public - per-clip values withheld pending publication

harmonic_mode.description: The tonal/harmonic framework governing the cue. Major and minor refer to diatonic functional harmony. Modal refers to non-diatonic mode usage (Dorian, Phrygian, Lydian, etc.) where modal color is a primary compositional decision. Atonal refers to non-tonal or deliberately non-functional harmonic structures.
harmonic_mode.annotation_guidance: Classify by compositional intent, not by automated key detection. A cue in D minor that uses Phrygian inflection as a primary color choice is annotated 'modal', not 'minor'.
harmonic_mode.research_relevance: RQ6: Is harmonic mode a primary or secondary predictor of emotional category? This field enables direct testing of the mode-emotion correlation hypothesis.
harmonic_mode.status: field structure public - per-clip values withheld pending publication

orchestration_density.description: Ordinal scale capturing the textural density of the orchestration. Proxy for the number and layering of active voices, harmonic complexity, and spatial fullness. Does not map directly to loudness or dynamic marking.

scale_definition.1: Solo or duo - single melodic voice or minimal harmonic support
scale_definition.2: Sparse ensemble - 3-6 voices, open texture, significant silence or space
scale_definition.3: Medium density - balanced ensemble, moderate harmonic layering
scale_definition.4: Dense - full ensemble engaged, complex harmonic layering, minimal silence
scale_definition.5: Saturated - maximum voices, full spectral range occupied, textural saturation

tempo_category.description: Qualitative tempo category assigned by the composer. Complements the computed BPM value from the feature extraction pipeline. 'Static' refers to cues with no discernible pulse (e.g., sustained pads, free rubato). 'Variable' refers to cues with significant tempo variation or ritardando.
tempo_category.status: field structure public - per-clip values withheld pending publication

narrative_function.description: The dramatic function of the cue within the scene. Captures compositional role beyond emotional content. Underscore: continuous emotional support. Stinger: short punctuation event. Theme statement: introduction or restatement of a named theme. Counterpoint: cue that works against the visual emotional tone. Transition: cue designed primarily for scene or act change. Source_music: diegetic placement.
narrative_function.status: field structure public - per-clip values withheld pending publication

composer_notes.description: Free-text field for compositional intent detail that does not fit structured fields. Documents edge cases, deliberate subversions of convention, inter-field tensions, or specific technical choices the composer considers emotionally significant. Selected entries from this field are included in the v0.25 public release.
composer_notes.status: selected non-disclosing entries released with v0.25

annotation_confidence.description: Annotator confidence in the primary emotion label. 3: high confidence, clear and stable compositional intent. 2: moderate confidence, some ambiguity or multi-category tension. 1: low confidence, the cue resists clean categorical assignment. Low-confidence annotations are retained in the corpus as theoretically informative edge cases.

edge_case_flags.description: Structured flags for annotation edge cases. Applied when the cue exhibits structural properties that challenge standard categorical assignment. Edge-case-flagged entries receive additional annotation scrutiny and are analyzed separately in EXP-004.
edge_case_flags.status: flag taxonomy public - per-clip assignments withheld pending publication

corpus_metadata.audio_format: 22050Hz, mono, float32 (features only - raw audio withheld)
corpus_metadata.corpus_composition_notes: Original compositions by Bernard G. Raw audio not committed to repository for IP protection.

citations.cite_as: Bernard G. (2026). Cinematic Emotion Annotation Schema v1.0. Cinematic Emotion Lab. https://github.com/bernardvgosh/cinematic-emotion-lab
